articleTitle: Comparison of overall survival across treatment modalities for oesophageal, gastroesophageal, and gastric cancer: protocol for a systematic review and network meta-analysis.
takeaways: This is a protocol paper outlining the methodology for a future systematic review and network meta-analysis, meaning no actual treatment outcomes or survival data have been analyzed yet

The study will compare survival outcomes across five treatment approaches: surgery alone, chemotherapy, combined chemoradiotherapy, immunotherapy, and multimodal treatment combinations for esophageal, gastroesophageal junction, and gastric cancers

The research addresses a current gap where treatment guidelines vary between these three related cancer types despite their similar characteristics, suggesting patients may receive different treatments depending on tumor location classification

The planned analysis will be the first comprehensive comparison of all available treatment modalities across this spectrum of upper digestive tract cancers, potentially informing more standardized treatment approaches
